sourceMovie.title: My Oni Girl
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: My Oni Girl works as a animation movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The animation and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Children Who Chase Lost Voices
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If My Oni Girl clicked for you, Children Who Chase Lost Voices (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and fantasy, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If My Oni Girl clicked for you, Pokémon the Movie: Hoopa and the Clash of Ages (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and fantasy, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after My Oni Girl?
faq.answer: Try Children Who Chase Lost Voices, Pokémon the Movie: Hoopa and the Clash of Ages, Spirited Away—each shares DNA with My Oni Girl while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is My Oni Girl?
faq.answer: My Oni Girl (2024) is best described as Animation, Fantasy, Adventure, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is My Oni Girl worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
